title: pyrus malus subsp. pumila
intro: Pyrus malus subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf or wild apple, is a subspecies of the common apple tree (Pyrus malus) that is native to Europe and Asia. The leaves and fruits of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus malus subsp. pumila have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
